title: Remotes, Refspecs, Pruning, and Safe Publication · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs remotes, refspecs, pruning, and safe publication from terse repository evidence, challenges one retained misconception, and revises the distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for distributed ref mapping and publication safety plan after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Parse positive and negative fetch and push refspecs, wildcard mappings, force markers, deletion, matching, and configured defaults.
Reason about advertised refs, negotiation, shallow or filtered boundaries, tag following, prune, prune-tags, and remote-tracking updates.
Predict fast-forward acceptance by namespace and object type and distinguish local preflight from server receive policy.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Separate evidence from confidence
steps.instruction:
review case — Case G96 is a review in a disposable repository. publication is safe only if remote main still equals the reviewed b00b1e5 lease value. The learner must predict the explicit source, destination, and expected old ID expose overwrite authority before mutation, run git push --dry-run --force-with-lease=refs/heads/main:b00b1e5 origin HEAD:refs/heads/main, explain the dry run accepts the exact mapping in simulation and records that no network changed, test this changed condition: advance remote main to c0ffee0 and require the explicit lease to reject publication, and preserve this recovery boundary: retain the old and proposed remote IDs plus a local recovery ref before authorized push. Competency probe: Capture remote URLs, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new IDs, server result, and post-fetch verification.

Inspect the Gita Multi Remote Publication trace for this review case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for capture remote urls, authentication boundary, exact source and destination, expected old and new ids, server result, and post-fetch verification.
